name: Cog (Containerize ML Models for Replicate)

auth.notes: REPLICATE_API_TOKEN for pushing to Replicate; local use requires Docker

performance.notes: Docker container startup; GPU inference on Replicate cloud

agent_readiness.notes: Package ML models as Docker containers for Replicate deployment. cog.yaml: build: python_version: 3.11; gpu: true; python_packages: [torch, transformers]. predict.py: from cog import BasePredictor, Input, Path. class Predictor(BasePredictor): def setup(self): self.model = load_model(). def predict(self, prompt: str = Input(description='Input prompt')) -> str: return self.model.generate(prompt). cog build → Docker image. cog push r8.im/user/model → Replicate. cog predict → local test. cog run python test.py. Streaming: yield from generator. File inputs: Path type. Output types: str, int, float, bool, Path, list. Auto-generated API schema.

gotchas: GPU inference on Replicate incurs costs
Container build time can be 5-30 minutes for large models
Replicate-specific deployment — not portable to arbitrary Kubernetes
Streaming output requires yield pattern in predict()
best_when: Packaging ML models for Replicate deployment with auto-generated API
Creating reproducible ML model containers with type-safe inputs/outputs
When Replicate's cloud GPU inference is the target platform
Sharing ML models publicly on Replicate with web demo
avoid_when: Non-Replicate deployments (use BentoML or LitServe for generic serving)
Production high-availability serving (use dedicated inference servers)
When model weights can't be open-sourced
summary: Cog packages ML models as Docker containers for Replicate deployment. cog.yaml declares GPU/Python deps. Predictor(BasePredictor): setup() + predict(prompt: str = Input()) → str. cog build + cog push r8.im/user/model. Auto-generated API schema. Streaming via yield. File I/O with Path type. Apache-2.0 by Replicate. Best for Replicate-targeted ML model packaging. Docker required. Container builds slow. Not for generic k8s deployments.
